places.name: Krivi Put
places.description: Zagreb's original craft beer bar with an ever-rotating tap list of Croatian and international microbrews.
places.local_story: Krivi Put literally means 'wrong way' — and that's exactly the point. The founders opened it when nobody in Zagreb cared about craft beer, tucked away on a side street most people would never find. Now it's the beating heart of the local craft scene, and half of Croatia's microbreweries got their start pouring here.
places.practical_tip: Ask the bartender what's new on tap — they always have something you can't find anywhere else. The garden out back is magical on summer evenings.

places.name: Swanky Monkey Garden
places.description: Part hostel bar, part urban jungle — a multi-level courtyard bar that feels like a secret treehouse in the city center.
places.local_story: What started as just a hostel bar became one of Zagreb's most iconic hangout spots. The courtyard is stacked vertically with platforms, plants everywhere, and string lights that make it feel like you've stumbled into someone's very cool backyard party. Locals come here just as much as travelers.
places.practical_tip: Grab a spot on the upper terrace for the best people-watching. Their cocktails are surprisingly good for a hostel bar — try the house spritz.

places.name: Bacchus Jazz Bar
places.description: A cozy basement jazz bar where the music is live, the wine list is Croatian, and time seems to stop.
places.local_story: This place has been a Zagreb institution for decades. It's the kind of bar where you go in for one glass of Graševina and somehow it's 2 AM and you're best friends with a saxophone player. The walls are covered in old jazz posters and the vibe is pure Donji Grad sophistication — without the pretension.
places.practical_tip: Come on Thursday or Friday for live jazz. Sit near the stage but not right next to the speaker. Order any Croatian white wine — they know their stuff.

places.name: Mr. Fogg
places.description: A speakeasy-style cocktail bar inspired by Jules Verne, hidden behind an unmarked door in the city center.
places.local_story: Finding Mr. Fogg is half the fun — there's no sign, just a door you'd walk right past. Inside it's all dark wood, vintage maps, and bartenders who take their craft seriously. It's where Zagreb's cocktail nerds go when they want something made properly. The menu changes seasonally and always has something you've never tried.
places.practical_tip: Look for the door on Petrinjska — ask a local if you can't find it. Tell the bartender what flavors you like and let them surprise you. Reservations help on weekends.

places.name: Tolkien's House
places.description: A fantasy-themed bar near the Stone Gate where every drink comes with a side of Middle-earth nostalgia.
places.local_story: Yes, it's themed — but it's done RIGHT. This isn't some tourist gimmick; it's run by genuine Tolkien fans who also happen to make excellent mead and craft cocktails. The interior feels like walking into Rivendell, and on winter evenings with candles lit, it's genuinely magical. Locals love it for the atmosphere and the surprisingly strong drink menu.
places.practical_tip: Order the mead — it's homemade and delicious. The upstairs room is cozier and quieter. Perfect pre-dinner spot when exploring Gornji Grad.

places.name: Alcatraz
places.description: A rock bar near Tkalčićeva with prison-themed decor, cheap drinks, and some of the best people-watching in Zagreb.
places.local_story: Alcatraz has been the go-to spot for Zagreb's alternative crowd for years. The drinks are cheap, the music is loud, and nobody cares what you're wearing. It sits right where Tkalčićeva meets the steps up to Gornji Grad, so you get this incredible mix of tourists wandering by and locals who've been coming here since forever. The terrace is prime real estate on warm nights.
places.practical_tip: Grab a terrace seat if you can — the Tkalčićeva people-watching is unbeatable. Their gemišt (white wine + sparkling water) is the cheapest in the area.

places.name: Spunk
places.description: A tiny, grungy dive bar that's become a cult favorite with Zagreb's music and art crowd.
places.local_story: Spunk is one of those places you either get or you don't. It's small, it's loud, the décor is chaotic (think concert posters, stickers, random art), and it has zero pretension. This is where musicians hang out after gigs, where graphic designers sketch on napkins, and where you'll have conversations with strangers that last until closing. If you want polished, go elsewhere. If you want real, you're home.
places.practical_tip: Don't expect table service — order at the bar. Check their Instagram for DJ nights and small gigs. Cash is preferred. The beer is cold and cheap.
